name: Tony (Tongyun) Yang
tagline: mean the limits of the world?
in more than we can name.
heroSpecialtyLine: Years across AI. Now I build in two places, on purpose.
openingHeadline: For years I roamed across AI, from emotion recognition and medical imaging to LLM routing and agentic benchmarking. Then I left my PhD fellowship on purpose. It landed like a sobering dose, and the haze burned off into one clear answer: I knew exactly what I wanted to build.
belief1: I want to build AI that is not only capable, but efficient, reliable, and useful.
belief2: AI is moving from a scaling race to a systems race. Larger models will still matter, but they are not enough. What matters is how we train, route, compress, deploy, and apply them under real constraints.
currently: An independent AI researcher in Amsterdam. I collaborate with industry and academia on cost-efficient large language models, optimization-as-reasoning, and world models. Earlier roles include Marie Skłodowska-Curie Fellow, AI Research Engineer at TU Delft Imaging Physics, and an MSc from TU Delft.
intro: Open to research, in academia or industry. What pulls me is meaning more than topic.

diptych.heading: Two directions
diptych.thesis: Two bets, one belief: build where data is sensitive and scarce, and turn frontier models into products people can actually use.
diptych.belief: I believe these two pave the next paradigm of AI.

directions.title: When the data doesn't exist, I build it
directions.blurb: I am drawn to the rooms public data never reaches, where it is sensitive, scarce, and costly to get wrong. Building there, from the ground up, is the work I love most.

directions.title: Frontier capability, made cheap enough to ship
directions.blurb: Frontier models are astonishing, and almost no one can afford to run them well. Closing that gap, until real people can actually use them, is the puzzle I keep coming back to.

researchDek: The receipts behind the two directions. Every paper here is one of those bets made concrete.
projectsIntro: Where the research meets real systems. Some are live collaborations (Gradient Networks, Tencent, MeetaVista); others I build on my own time. Most are still in motion.

businessCard.name: Tony Yang
businessCard.looking: Open to research · academia or industry
businessCard.tagline: Sensitive, scarce-data AI · frontier models made shippable

contactInvitation: I read everything. Reach out about research, collaboration, supervision, or just to say hi. I answer faster than is professional, and I bring my whole self to the conversation.
